title: Compliance Classification Column
description: Schema for the per-site compliance classification column in crawl data. Each object represents one site's compliance status across all four tracked privacy string types: USPS, OptanonConsent, GPP, and Well-known. A 'null' value for any section means the site had no detectable implementation of that privacy string (before or after receiving a GPC signal) and therefore receives no classification for it. Only sites that have at least one privacy string detected — before, after, or both, even if invalid — will receive a classification for that string.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this schema file.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this schema format, e.g. '1.0.0'. Increment the major version for any breaking change to field names or enum values.

site.description: The domain of the site this record applies to (e.g. 'example.com').

usps.description: Classification for the US Privacy String (USPS). Derived by comparing uspapi_before_gpc / usp_cookies_before_gpc against uspapi_after_gpc / usp_cookies_after_gpc. See the USPS Compliance Classification table for full condition-to-status mapping.

oneOf.description: Null: no detectable USPS present before or after GPC — no classification assigned.

optanonConsent.description: Classification for the OneTrust OptanonConsent cookie. Derived by comparing the isGpcEnabled field in OptanonConsent_before_gpc vs OptanonConsent_after_gpc. Note: 'not_applicable' does not exist for OptanonConsent — the cookie format provides no mechanism to declare non-applicability. See the OptanonConsent Compliance Classification table for full condition-to-status mapping.

oneOf.description: Null: no detectable OptanonConsent cookie present before or after GPC — no classification assigned.

wellKnown.description: Classification for the /.well-known/gpc.json resource. Single-point-in-time evaluation — there is no before/after comparison; only the content found after GPC is evaluated. 'invalid' means the file exists but neither 'gpc: true' nor 'gpc: false' can be parsed from it. 'not_applicable' does not exist for Well-known. See the Well-known Compliance Classification table for full condition-to-status mapping.

oneOf.description: Null: no /.well-known/gpc.json file was served — no classification assigned.

gpp.description: Classification(s) for the Global Privacy Platform (GPP) String. GPP produces multiple classifications — one per (state, field) combination where data was detected. 'state' follows GPP spec section identifiers: 'US' for US National, two-letter abbreviations for state-level sections. 'field' is one of the three opt-out fields. 'status' is the outcome for that (state, field) pair. Derived by comparing decoded_gpp_before_gpc vs decoded_gpp_after_gpc. A site is considered to have a missing GPP String if sections do not match before and after opt-out. See the GPP Compliance Classification table and the Legend for full details.

classifications.description: One entry per (state, field) combination where GPP data was detected. Must have at least one item — if no GPP data was detected at all, 'gpp' should be null.

state.description: GPP section identifier. 'US' = US National. State codes (CA, CT, CO, TX, VA, OR, MT, DE, IA, NH, NJ, TN, etc.) match the GPP spec.

field.description: The opt-out field within the GPP section being classified.

status.description: The GPP compliance outcome for this (state, field) pair.

oneOf.description: Null: no GPP String detected in any state section before or after GPC — no classification assigned.
